reasoning: The user provided a news article about Tawel Fan ward and asks for a concise summary. I will produce a neutral, compact summary that captures the key facts: closure, potential contributory deaths, ongoing investigations, unpublished mortality review, family perspectives, and the broader impact on the health board.
output: Tawel Fan ward at Glan Clwyd Hospital in Denbighshire was closed more than three years ago after revelations that some patients were treated poorly, with at least seven families told that treatment may have contributed to deaths. The Betsi Cadwaladr University Health Board says an independent investigation by Health and Social Care Advisory Service (Hascas) is under way and that the quality of care could have been a contributory factor in some deaths. A mortality-rate review for the ward has reportedly been completed but not published. Relatives say lessons have not been learned and questions remain, despite an apology from the health board acknowledging problems in care. The Tawel Fan scandal contributed to special measures for the board, costing about £5 million annually. An initial report was published almost three years ago, with two more reports due later this year, including details of a mortality review; watchdogs urge swift release, and the board says each family will receive a personal report to inform the Tawel Fan investigation.
